div1.entryFree:
Rank, vb. 1) tr. a) to place with respect to degree and dignity: my fortunes (are) as fairly --ed as Demetrius', Mids. I, 1, 101. I will not r. me with the barbarous multitudes, Merch. II, 9, 33. he was a man of an unbounded stomach, ever --ing himself with princes, H8 IV, 2, 34. let the world r. me in register a master-leaver, Ant. IV, 9, 21. b) to match, to couple: to r. our chosen truth with such a show as fool and fight is, Ant. IV, 9, 21. if sour woe delights in fellowship and needly will be --ed with other griefs, Rom. III, 2, 117.
2) intr. to be coupled, to go together: let that one article r. with the rest, H5 V, 2, 374.
